sections.content: C is a general-purpose, procedural programming language developed by Dennis Ritchie in 1972 at Bell Laboratories. It is widely used for system programming, application development, embedded systems, and operating systems.
C was developed by Dennis Ritchie as an improvement over the B language. It became one of the most influential programming languages and inspired many modern languages such as C++, Java, and C#.
C is a structured programming language that allows programmers to write efficient and portable programs. It provides low-level memory access while maintaining high-level programming features.

content: Provides direct access to memory through pointers
Widely used in system software development
Foundation for many modern programming languages
main() is the entry point of program execution.
printf() displays output on the screen.
Forms the foundation of many programming languages
Widely used in academia and industry

summary: C is a general-purpose procedural programming language.
Dennis Ritchie developed C at Bell Laboratories.
C is fast, portable, and efficient.
C is widely used in operating systems, embedded systems, and software development.
Understanding C helps in learning advanced programming concepts.
